items.title: 5 Dividend Stocks to Hold for the Next 5 Years
items.author: Will Healy
items.description: As retirement approaches, investors seek income-producing assets with safe, growing dividends. Five companies offer attractive dividend yields and potential market-beating returns, including Target, Realty Income, IBM, PepsiCo, and AT&T.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Facing supply chain and DEI challenges, but maintains a 54-year dividend growth streak with a 4.3% yield
Offers monthly dividends with consistent annual increases, 5.5% yield, and potential recovery as interest rates fall
Pivoted to AI and cloud, doubled stock price, maintains 30-year dividend growth, but limited revenue growth
Diverse brand portfolio, 53-year dividend growth streak, 3.7% yield, and recent stock price recovery
Recovered from dividend cut, refocused on core wireless business, 3.9% yield, and stock price improvement

items.title: Enterprise Software Extinction Via AI Exaggerated, Their Stocks Becoming Incredibly Cheap
items.description: Enterprise software is not facing extinction from generative AI. Click here to read more about how incumbents are adapting and leveraging AI themselves.
